heading: Appeals, notice, hearing, and mediation; alternative agency for Indian tribe
label: 9841 Appeals, notice, hearing, and mediation; alternative agency for Indian tribe

children.heading: Notice requirements; suspension or termination of assistance stayed pending hearing; mediation
children.label: a Notice requirements; suspension or termination of assistance stayed pending hearing; mediation

children.text: procedures to assure that special notice of and an opportunity for a timely and expeditious appeal to the Secretary will be provided for an agency or organization which desires to serve as a delegate agency under this subchapter and whose application to the Head Start agency has been wholly or substantially rejected or has not been acted upon within a period of time deemed reasonable by the Secretary, in accordance with regulations which the Secretary shall prescribe;

procedures to assure that financial assistance under this subchapter shall not be suspended, except in emergency situations, unless the recipient agency has been given reasonable notice and opportunity to show cause why such action should not be taken;

children.label: A
children.text: a right to file a notice of appeal of a decision not later than 30 days after notice of the decision from the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: access to a full and fair hearing of the appeal, not later than 120 days after receipt by the Secretary of the notice of appeal;

children.label: i
children.text: recipients of financial assistance under this subchapter; and

children.label: ii
children.text: delegate agencies, or policy councils of Head Start agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: avoid the need for an administrative hearing on an adverse action; and

children.label: C
children.text: prohibit a Head Start agency from expending financial assistance awarded under this subchapter for the purpose of paying legal fees, or other costs incurred, pursuant to an appeal under paragraph (3);

children.label: A
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (B), for not more than 30 days; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a recipient under this subchapter that has multiple and recurring deficiencies for 180 days or more and has not made substantial and significant progress toward meeting the goals of the grantee’s quality improvement plan or eliminating all deficiencies identified by the Secretary, during the hearing of an appeal described in paragraph (3), for any amount of time; and

procedures to assure that in cases where a Head Start agency prevails in a decision under paragraph (4), the Secretary may determine and provide a reimbursement to the Head Start agency for fees deemed reasonable and customary.

children.heading: Notification of conflict by Head Start agency to regional office
children.label: b Notification of conflict by Head Start agency to regional office

children.text: the date by which a Head Start agency engaged in a conflict described in subsection (a)(4) will notify the appropriate regional office of the Department of the conflict; and
a reasonable period for the mediation.
a timeline for an administrative hearing, if necessary, on an adverse action; and
a timeline by which the person conducting the administrative hearing shall issue a decision based on the hearing.

children.heading: Termination of designation not stayed upon appeal
children.label: d Termination of designation not stayed upon appeal
children.text: In any case in which a termination, reduction, or suspension of financial assistance under this subchapter is upheld in an administrative hearing under this section, such termination, reduction, or suspension shall not be stayed pending any judicial appeal of such administrative decision.

children.heading: Establishment of alternative agency by Indian tribe
children.label: e Establishment of alternative agency by Indian tribe
A
children.text: the Secretary terminates financial assistance under this section to the only agency that was receiving financial assistance to provide Head Start services to the Indian tribe; and

children.label: B
children.text: the tribe would otherwise be precluded from providing such services to the members of the tribe.

children.label: A
children.text: served on the administrative staff or program staff of the agency described in paragraph (1)(A); and

children.label: i
children.text: relates to the performance standards or financial management standards described in ; andsection 9836a(a)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: was the basis for the termination of financial assistance described in paragraph (1)(A);
